capabilities.name: Tiered quantity discounts
capabilities.verify: multiple pricelist_items.min_quantity tiers per product; resolve_pricelist_price picks the deepest qualifying break (ORDER BY specificity, qty_break DESC) (migration 20260613020000, re-emitted forward-dated 20260704182000 to defeat ledger drift)
capabilities.notes: LIVE VERIFICATION FOUND A DRIFT BUG 2026-07-04: setup verified (created pricelist restricted to a test lead + two tiers via manage_pricelist/manage_pricelist_item, cleaned up), but resolve_pricelist_price failed at EVERY quantity with 'column reference "price_cents" is ambiguous'. Root cause is NOT the code — the correct ambiguity-free body (qualified pr.price_cents, output aliased to resolved_price) already exists in migration 20260613020000, but that file is back-dated below the managed instance's migration-ledger HEAD and was silently skipped, so rzhj still runs an older broken body. This is the exact forward-date-drift class from CLAUDE.md. Fix: forward-dated CREATE OR REPLACE 20260704182000_fix-resolve-pricelist-price-drift.sql. NOTE: the same drift means the sibling 'price_resolution' capability (marked done) is also broken on drifted instances until this deploys — the fix restores both. Stays partial pending live re-verify after deploy (Lovable MCP down at fix time). Scratch-Postgres had verified the logic 2026-06-13 (qty 5→10000, 12→9000@10%, 80→7500@25%) — a clean case of correct code that never reached the instance.
